text:
First, note that time zones as we know them (like Europe/Paris and US/Pacific) did not exist in 1611.  
But if we treat this as a modern time zone conversion ignoring historical changes:  
- Europe/Paris is UTC+1 (or UTC+2 during DST, but DST didn’t exist in 1611; assume standard time).  
- US/Pacific is UTC-8 (standard time).  
- Time difference: UTC+1 to UTC-8 is 9 hours behind Paris.  

9:00 in Paris → subtract 9 hours → 0:00 same day in US/Pacific.  

Since subtracting 9 hours from 9 AM goes back to midnight, the date remains June 20, 1611.  

FINAL_ANSWER: 00:00 June 20, 1611
